# Rejected Fuzzy Match Examples
#
# These are pairs where semantic similarity may be high BUT context changes meaning.
# Format: source_text | candidate_translation | expected: REJECTED
# These MUST be rejected by the context guard even if similarity >= threshold

# 1. Negation flip - completely opposite meaning
"He is happy." | "He is not happy." | REJECTED

# 2. Subject/object swap - changes who does what
"The dog bit the man." | "The man bit the dog." | REJECTED

# 3. Tense change - changes temporal meaning
"He walks to school." | "He walked to school." | REJECTED

# 4. Number change - changes quantity
"There are 3 apples." | "There are 5 apples." | REJECTED

# 5. Pronoun reference change - changes referent
"He gave it to her." | "She gave it to him." | REJECTED

# 6. Modal verb change - changes certainty/obligation
"You must go." | "You may go." | REJECTED

# 7. Proper noun substitution - changes entity
"Tokyo is the capital." | "Osaka is the capital." | REJECTED

# 8. Adjective antonym - opposite quality
"The water is hot." | "The water is cold." | REJECTED

# 9. Preposition change - changes spatial relation
"The book is on the table." | "The book is under the table." | REJECTED

# 10. Quantifier change - changes scope
"All students passed." | "Some students passed." | REJECTED

# 11. Japanese: negation flip
"行きます。" | "行きません。" | REJECTED

# 12. Japanese: subject/topic change
"田中さんが来ました。" | "佐藤さんが来ました。" | REJECTED

# 13. Japanese: tense change
"食べました。" | "食べます。" | REJECTED

# 14. Japanese: honorific level change (changes social relationship)
"先生が来られました。" | "先生が来た。" | REJECTED

# 15. Japanese: particle change (wa/ga) changing focus
"私は学生です。" | "私が学生です。" | REJECTED

# 16. Japanese: verb form changing aspect
"食べている。" | "食べた。" | REJECTED

# 17. Japanese: potential form vs plain (ability vs action)
"読めます。" | "読みます。" | REJECTED

# 18. Japanese: causative vs passive (who causes what)
"先生に読ませた。" | "先生に読まれた。" | REJECTED

# 19. Japanese: conditional vs statement
"雨なら行く。" | "雨だ。" | REJECTED

# 20. Japanese: desire vs statement
"行きたい。" | "行く。" | REJECTED
